title: A Yorkshire Tragedy
summaries: "A Yorkshire Tragedy" by William Shakespeare is a domestic tragedy written in 1605. This dark Jacobean play tells the harrowing story of a Yorkshire gentleman who descends into ruin through gambling and vice. Based on the real-life crimes of Walter Calverley, who murdered his own children, the play explores how one man's moral collapse destroys his family. Originally performed by Shakespeare's company at the Globe Theatre, this brief but brutal work examines domestic violence and financial ruin with unflinching intensity, though modern scholars attribute it to Thomas Middleton rather than Shakespeare.
